steps.title: Use the residents' praise
steps.content: The residents defend the city by pointing to its institutions, social events, and pleasant families. They always identify the Turkins as the most accomplished and enlightened family, which gives the Turkins a special standing in the city.

steps.title: Reject unsupported inferences
steps.content:
• Choice A says the Turkins are newcomers, but the text describes how residents present them.
  • Choice C says they are amused by other residents, which is not stated.
  • Choice D says they dislike the city, which the text never suggests.
The remaining choice captures their unique status in the city. The answer is B.
